title: Step 4: Update the snapshot copy policy.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To modify or delete backup policies, you must have
[Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
access to the project. Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>)
access must add themselves as a `Project Backup Manager` or
`Project Owner` to the project before they can modify or delete
backup policies.

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
- If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy) enabled
  with the **Keep all snapshots when removing additional
  snapshot regions** option set to **On** and you modify or
  delete a snapshot policy item, any existing snapshots are retained
  until their scheduled expiration date.

- If you [Change the region into which a cluster is deployed](scale-cluster-region) to a secondary region that you currently
  have configured for snapshot copy distribution, Atlas
  automatically updates your backup policy to distribute snapshots
  copies from your new primary region to the original primary region
  (now a secondary region) and other secondary regions configured for
  snapshot distribution.

- If you enable a [VPC/VNet peering](vpc-peering) and
  restrict certain regions for Google Cloud, you can copy snapshots to only
  those regions.

- Oplog copy distribution is enabled at the copy region level. If you
  set **Point-in-Time Restore** to **On** for a
  snapshot copy policy item, Atlas copies oplogs to that copy
  region and enables [Restore from Continuous Cloud Backup](restore-from-continuous) for all snapshot copy policies in that
  copy region.

sections.heading: Enable Snapshot Copy Distribution
sections.body:
To enable and configure snapshot copy distribution from your primary
region to other regions using the Atlas UI:

This displays a table where you can configure snapshot copy policy
items to distribute snapshot copies to additional regions.

> **Note:** For a global cluster, you can enable or disable this setting
> independently for each zone.

To copy snapshots to all secondary regions in a multi-region
cluster, toggle Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
to On.

When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster
Regions, Atlas configures snapshot copy
policy items for each secondary region in your cluster and keeps
the policy in sync with the cluster configuration as you add or
remove secondary regions. Atlas creates each snapshot copy
policy item with the same frequency and retention times as the
backup policy items defined for the cluster.

You can customize the frequency and retention times for the
automatic snapshot copies in the following steps, but you cannot
add or remove regions unless you disable this setting.

In the Region column of the snapshot copy policy
table, select a region from the Choose a region dropdown.

To add another region, click Add Another Region and
Frequency.

> **Note:** You must select copy regions from within the same cloud provider as
> the primary region of your cluster. For example, if your cluster's
> [highest priority region](manual/reference/glossary) is set to the AWS (Amazon Web Services) region
> `us-east-1`, you can only distribute snapshot copies to other AWS (Amazon Web Services)
> regions supported by Atlas.
>
> If you have a global cluster, each zone has its own primary
> region. You must select copy regions within the same cloud
> provider as the primary region for each zone.

> **Note:** If Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
> is set to On, you can't add or delete copy regions,
> but you can modify the frequency and retention times for the snapshot
> copy policy items that Atlas automatically configures for each
> secondary region in your cluster.

In the Snapshots column for each region that you
added, select the frequency for copying snapshots to that region.

You can select from the following frequency options:

- Last number of snapshots: Copies a specific number
  of the most recent snapshots to the copy region, deleting older
  snapshots as new snapshots are copied. If you select this
  option, you can't select any other frequency options for that
  region.

  For example, specifying Last `5`
  Snapshots copies the five most recent snapshots
  taken on the source region, and maintains that number of copies
  for as long as the copy policy item is active.

- Backup Policy Frequency: Copies snapshots taken
  according to the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy)
  item(s) defined for your cluster. Possible values include
  `Hourly`, `Daily`, `Weekly`, `Monthly`, or `Yearly`.

  The available frequencies correspond to the frequencies of the
  backup policy items defined for your cluster. For example, if
  your backup policy specifies only weekly and monthly snapshots,
  then you can select either `Weekly` or `Monthly` as the
  snapshot copy frequency to copy those respective snapshots to
  the copy region.

  Atlas copies snapshots taken for *all* backup policy items
  with the specified frequency. For example, if your backup policy
  takes two weekly snapshots, then a `Weekly` snapshot copy
  policy item copies both weekly snapshots.

  To select more than one frequency option for the same copy
  region, click Add Another Region and Frequency and
  choose the same region in the new row.

  > **Note:** If overlapping policy items generate the same snapshot, Atlas
  > associates the snapshot with the policy item with the longest retention
  > time.
  >
  > For example, if the policy specifies a daily snapshot with two-day
  > retention and a weekly snapshot every Saturday with three-week
  > retention, then Atlas displays a frequency of Weekly in
  > the Frequency column on the Snapshots page for
  > the snapshot taken on Saturday and retains the snapshot for three weeks.
  > This is because the weekly policy item has a longer retention time than
  > the daily policy item.

- On Demand: Copies on-demand snapshots
  taken for the cluster.

In the Retention Time column for each row that you
added, select the retention time for snapshots copied to that
region and frequency. The retention time cannot exceed the
retention time set

You can choose from the following units of time for retention:

- Hours

- Days

- Weeks

- Months

In the Point-in-Time Restore column for each row that
you added, set the toggle to On to enable oplog copy
distribution to that region. This enables PIT (Point in Time) restores for all
snapshot copies in that region.

This is only available if you enabled Continuous Cloud Backup for your
cluster.

Review the new snapshot copy policy configuration in the dialog
box, and click Confirm to save your changes.

To enable and configure snapshot copy distribution from your primary
region to other regions using the Atlas Administration API:

Use the [Get Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint to retrieve the current
Cloud Backup schedule for the cluster. The `copySettings`
field in the response body contains the current snapshot copy
policy.

Copy the entire response to modify in the next step.

> **Important:** If the `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` value in the response is `false`,
> set it to `true` to configure snapshot copy policy items using the
> `copyPolicyItems` attribute in the next step. To do this:
>
> - Copy and modify the response to set `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` to
>   `true`.
>
> - Make a `PATCH` request to the same endpoint with the modified
>   payload.
>
> The `copyPolicyItems` attribute replaces the deprecated
> `frequencies` attribute and allows you to define snapshot copy
> policy items with custom retention values. If you keep
> `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` set to `false`, you must use the
> deprecated `frequencies` attribute to specify which snapshots to
> copy. These copies are automatically retained for the same retention
> period as the source snapshots.

To distribute snapshot copies to all secondary regions in a
multi-region cluster, set the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field to
`true` and send the modified payload to the
[Update Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint to update the
snapshot copy policy.

When you set `autoCopySettingsEnabled` to `true`, Atlas
configures snapshot copy policy items for each secondary region in
your cluster and keeps the policy in sync with the cluster
configuration as you add or remove secondary regions. Atlas
creates each snapshot copy policy item with the same frequency and
retention times as the backup policy items defined for the
cluster.

To customize the frequency and retention times for the automatic
snapshot copies, copy the response from this step to modify in the
next step. If the automatic snapshot copy policy meets your needs,
you can end the procedure here.

Modify the response from the last step to update the
`copySettings` array with your desired snapshot copy policy
items.

Each item in the `copySettings` array represents the active
snapshot copy policy items for a specified region in a zone. For
an example snapshot copy policy configuration, see
[Update Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

> **Note:** You must select copy regions from within the same cloud provider as
> the primary region of your cluster. For example, if your cluster's
> [highest priority region](manual/reference/glossary) is set to the AWS (Amazon Web Services) region
> `us-east-1`, you can only distribute snapshot copies to other AWS (Amazon Web Services)
> regions supported by Atlas.
>
> If you have a global cluster, each zone has its own primary
> region. You must select copy regions within the same cloud
> provider as the primary region for each zone.

> **Note:** If the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field is set to `true`, you
> can't add or delete copy regions, but you can modify the frequency
> and retention times for the snapshot copy policy items that
> Atlas automatically configures for each secondary region in
> your cluster.

Make a request to the [Update Cloud Backup Schedule
for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint
with the modified payload to update the snapshot copy policy.

sections.heading: Update Snapshot Copy Policy
sections.body:
To update the snapshot copy policy for your cluster using the
Atlas UI:

If Copy to other regions is enabled, this section
displays a table of your currently configured snapshot copy
policies.

Toggle Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster
Regions to On or Off to enable or disable
automatic distribution of snapshot copies to all secondary regions
in a multi-region cluster.

When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions with
Cluster Regions, Atlas configures snapshot copy policy
items for each secondary region in your cluster and keeps the
policy in sync with the cluster configuration as you add or remove
secondary regions. Atlas creates each snapshot copy policy
item with the same frequency and retention times as the backup
policy items defined for the cluster.

> **Important:** When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions
> with Cluster Regions, Atlas overwrites any existing
> snapshot copy policy items you have configured.

Make your changes to the snapshot copy policy items in the table.

To add a new snapshot copies policy item, click Add
Another Region and Frequency and configure the new item in the
new row.

> **Note:** If Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
> is set to On, you can't add or delete copy regions,
> but you can modify the frequency and retention times for the snapshot
> copy policy items that Atlas automatically configures for each
> secondary region in your cluster.

A dialog box appears, summarizing your new snapshot copies policy
configuration. If you made changes, you can select checkboxes for
the following options to apply your changes to existing snapshots
in the copy regions:

- Update retention time of existing snapshot copies

- Delete all snapshots from removed policies

> **Note:** Updates made with this method only apply to snapshot copies
> created after February 11, 2026. To update snapshot copies
> created before this date, you must use the `updateSnapshots`
> option in a `PATCH` request to the Atlas Administration API
> [/backup/schedule](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2)
> endpoint.

To update the snapshot copy policy for your cluster using the
Atlas Administration API:

Use the [Get Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint to retrieve the current
Cloud Backup schedule for the cluster. The `copySettings`
field in the response body contains the current snapshot copy p
olicy.

Copy the entire response to modify in the next step.

> **Important:** If the `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` value in the response is `false`,
> set it to `true` to configure snapshot copy policy items using the
> `copyPolicyItems` attribute in the next step. To do this:
>
> - Copy and modify the response to set `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` to
>   `true`.
>
> - Make a `PATCH` request to the same endpoint with the modified
>   payload.
>
> The `copyPolicyItems` attribute replaces the deprecated
> `frequencies` attribute and allows you to define snapshot copy
> policy items with custom retention values. If you keep
> `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` set to `false`, you must use the
> deprecated `frequencies` attribute to specify which snapshots to
> copy. These copies are automatically retained for the same retention
> period as the source snapshots.

Set the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field to `true` or `false`
and send the modified payload to the [Update Cloud
Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint to enable or disable
automatic distribution of snapshot copies to all secondary regions
in a multi-region cluster.

When you set `autoCopySettingsEnabled` to `true`, Atlas
configures snapshot copy policy items for each secondary region in
your cluster and keeps the policy in sync with the cluster
configuration as you add or remove secondary regions. Atlas
creates each snapshot copy policy item with the same frequency and
retention times as the backup policy items defined for the
cluster.

To customize the frequency and retention times for the automatic
snapshot copies, copy the response from this step to modify in the
next step. If the automatic snapshot copy policy meets your needs,
you can end the procedure here.

> **Important:** When you set the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field to `true`,
> Atlas overwrites any existing snapshot copy policy items
> you have configured.

Modify the response from the last step to update the
`copySettings` array with your desired snapshot copy policy
items.

Each item in the `copySettings` array represents the active
snapshot copy policy items for a specified region in a zone. For
an example snapshot copy policy configuration, see
[Update Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

> **Note:** If the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field is set to `true`, you
> can't add or delete copy regions, but you can modify the frequency
> and retention times for the snapshot copy policy items that
> Atlas automatically configures for each secondary region in
> your cluster.

You can pass the following parameters into the modified payload to
apply your updated snapshot copy policy to existing snapshot
copies:

- `updateCopySnapshots`: Pass in this parameter with a value of
  `true` to update the retention time of existing snapshot copies
  to match the new retention time set for the corresponding
  snapshot policy item.

- `deleteCopySnapshots`: Pass in this parameter with a value of
  `true` to delete all snapshots from removed policies.

> **Note:** Updates made with this method only apply to snapshot copies
> created after February 11, 2026. To update snapshot copies
> created before this date, you must use the `updateSnapshots`
> option in a `PATCH` request to the Atlas Administration API
> [/backup/schedule](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2)
> endpoint.

Make a request to the [Update Cloud Backup Schedule
for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint
with the modified payload to update the snapshot copy policy.

sections.heading: Disable Snapshot Copy Distribution
sections.body:
To disable snapshot copy distribution or delete snapshot copy policy
items in the Atlas UI:

This displays a table of your currently configured snapshot copy
policies.

To disable all snapshot copy distribution for the cluster, toggle
Copy to other regions to Off.

If you want to disable only specific snapshot copy policy items,
click  in the Action column for the
region and frequency you want to stop copying snapshots for.

> **Note:** For a global cluster, you can enable or disable the
> Copy to other regions setting independently for
> each zone.

> **Note:** You must set Automatically Sync Copy Regions with
> Cluster Regions to Off to be able to add or delete
> copy regions. When this setting is on, Atlas
> automatically configures snapshot copy policy items for each
> secondary region in your cluster and keeps the policy in sync
> with the cluster configuration as you add or remove secondary
> regions.

A dialog box appears summarizing your new snapshot copy policy
configuration. If you disabled snapshot copy distribution or
deleted any snapshot copy policy items, you can select from the
following options to apply your changes to existing snapshots in
the copy regions:

- Update retention time of existing snapshot copies

- Delete all snapshots from removed policies

> **Note:** Updates made with this method only apply to snapshot copies created
> after February 11, 2026. To delete snapshot copies created before
> this date, you must use the `deleteCopiedBackups` option in a
> `PATCH` request to the Atlas Administration API
> [/backup/schedule](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2)
> endpoint. Alternatively, you can delete the original snapshots
> associated with the snapshot copies to delete the copies.

To disable snapshot copy distribution or delete snapshot copy policy
items using the Atlas Administration API:

Use the [Get Cloud Backup Schedule for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint to retrieve the current
Cloud Backup schedule for the cluster. The `copySettings`
field in the response body contains the current snapshot copy
policy.

Copy the entire response to modify in the next step.

> **Important:** If the `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` value in the response is `false`,
> set it to `true` to configure snapshot copy policy items using the
> `copyPolicyItems` attribute in the next step. To do this:
>
> - Copy and modify the response to set `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` to
>   `true`.
>
> - Make a `PATCH` request to the same endpoint with the modified
>   payload.
>
> The `copyPolicyItems` attribute replaces the deprecated
> `frequencies` attribute and allows you to define snapshot copy
> policy items with custom retention values. If you keep
> `copyPolicyItemsEnabled` set to `false`, you must use the
> deprecated `frequencies` attribute to specify which snapshots to
> copy. These copies are automatically retained for the same retention
> period as the source snapshots.

Modify the response from the previous step to remove the copy
policy item(s) that you want to disable from the `copySettings`
array.

To disable all snapshot copy distribution, replace the
`copySettings` array with an empty array.

> **Note:** You must set the `autoCopySettingsEnabled` field to `false`
> to be able to add or delete copy regions. When
> `autoCopySettingsEnabled` is `true`, Atlas
> automatically configures snapshot copy policy items for each
> secondary region in your cluster and keeps the policy in sync
> with the cluster configuration as you add or remove secondary
> regions.

To delete all copy snapshots associated with removed policies,
pass in the `deleteCopySnapshots` parameter with a value of
`true` in the modified payload.

> **Note:** Updates made with this method only apply to snapshot copies created
> after February 11, 2026. To delete snapshot copies created before
> this date, you must use the `deleteCopiedBackups` option in a
> `PATCH` request to the Atlas Administration API
> [/backup/schedule](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2)
> endpoint. Alternatively, you can delete the original snapshots
> associated with the snapshot copies to delete the copies.

Make a request to the [Update Cloud Backup Schedule
for One Cluster](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) endpoint with
the modified payload to update the snapshot copy policy.

sections.heading: Example Snapshot Copy Policy
sections.body:
This example snapshot copy policy distributes copies of snapshots taken
for the following backup policy:

**Backup Policy:**

This example [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) takes
hourly, daily, and weekly snapshots and stores them in the same region
as the cluster's primary node.

| Frequency Unit  | Every   | Retention Time | Snapshot Time |
| --------------- | ------- | -------------- | ------------- |
| Hourly Snapshot | 6 hours | 2 days         | 18:30 UTC     |
| Daily Snapshot  | N/A     | 7 days         | 18:30 UTC     |
| Weekly Snapshot | Monday  | 4 weeks        | 18:30 UTC     |
| Weekly Snapshot | Friday  | 4 weeks        | 18:30 UTC     |

**Snapshot Copy Policy:**

This snapshot copy policy copies daily and hourly snapshots to the AWS (Amazon Web Services)
region `us-east-2` (Ohio), and copies all weekly snapshots to
`us-west-1` (N. California). It retains each snapshot copy in the copy
regions for the specified retention time, and copies oplogs to the
`us-east-2` region to enable PIT (Point in Time) restores.

| Region           | Snapshots | Retention Time | Point in Time Restore |
| ---------------- | --------- | -------------- | --------------------- |
| us-east-2 (Copy) | Hourly    | 12 hours       | Enabled               |
| us-east-2 (Copy) | Daily     | 7 days         | Enabled               |
| us-west-1 (Copy) | Weekly    | 7 days         | Disabled              |
